title: Ipomoea daltonii
taxonomy: Ipomoea daltonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family, Convolvulaceae. It is a member of the genus Ipomoea, which includes over 600 species of flowering plants. Ipomoea daltonii is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is a climbing vine with heart-shaped leaves and funnel-shaped flowers, typically in shades of blue or purple. In terms of its taxonomic classification, Ipomoea daltonii is often placed in the section Quamoclit.

intro: Ipomoea daltonii, also known as Dalton's morning glory, is a flowering vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and gastrointestinal issues. Bioactive constituents of Ipomoea daltonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in infusions and decoctions to support the treatment of various health conditions.
